1.name: Rosewood Miramar Beach
1.notes: Ultra-luxury beachfront resort with private beach access, spa, and fine dining. Located west of downtown with stunning coastal views.

1.name: Four Seasons Resort The Biltmore Santa Barbara
1.notes: Historic oceanfront luxury property with lush gardens, multiple pools, and Michelin-starred dining options in Montecito.

1.name: Santa Barbara Hillside House
1.notes: Intimate luxury inn with contemporary design, ocean views, and personalized service near State Street shopping and dining.

1.name: Belmond El Encanto
1.notes: Elegant hillside retreat with gardens, spa, and fine dining overlooking the city and Pacific Ocean.

3.name: Bed Bath & Beyond
3.notes: Full selection of bedding, pillows, towels, and dorm essentials. Typically offers student discounts during move-in season.

3.name: Target
3.notes: Wide range of affordable dorm items including storage bins, desk organizers, lighting, and décor. Often has dedicated back-to-college sections.

3.name: Walmart
3.notes: Budget-friendly option for basics like bedding, hangers, cleaning supplies, and electronics accessories.

3.name: IKEA
3.notes: Compact furniture and storage solutions perfect for dorm rooms, plus affordable décor and organizational items.

3.name: Local Home Depot or Lowe's
3.notes: Hardware store option for items like power strips, extension cords, and any last-minute repairs or modifications.

1: Book a spa treatment at sunset to recover from travel
Dinner reservations at The Biltmore or Rosewood restaurants for arrival night
Request ocean-view rooms for morning views before departure to Hudson River
3: Shop early morning on move-in day to beat crowds and ensure full stock of popular items
Bring a detailed packing list and dorm room dimensions to avoid over-purchasing
Check if your college has a move-in essentials list or partnerships with specific retailers for discounts
Consider purchasing bulky items (pillows, mattress pad) before arriving to save luggage space

title: Take Hudson to College
subtitle: Move into UCSB
itineraryNotes:
Gay’s picks for hotels:

For your travel project, we need a hotel to take Hudson for school. I looked at a few hotels though the amex website and these are all acceptable to me but I figured you could book and use your app.  Acceptable

https://www.kirkwoodcollection.com/hideaway/rooms/

https://www.ihg.com/kimptonhotels/hotels/us/en/canary-hotel-santa-barbara-ca/sabcn/hoteldetail

https://www.marriott.com/en-us/hotels/sbatx-the-steward-santa-barbara-a-tribute-portfolio-hotel/overview/

https://www.hotelcalifornian.com/

I think we should arrive on the 17th of sept, run our purchase errands on the 18th and pick up supplies and then we are moving in on the 19th & 20th.  He will get a staggered time for move in on one of those dates? So I supposed we can fly home late on the 20th pretty safely?
